laws.title: North Carolina Minimum Wage — $7.25/hr (Federal Rate)
laws.summary: North Carolina's minimum wage equals the federal minimum of $7.25 per hour. There is no state-level increase above the federal rate, and no increase is currently scheduled. Tipped employees may be paid $2.13/hour by the employer provided tips bring total compensation to at least $7.25/hour.
compliance_actions: Pay all non-exempt employees at least $7.25/hour
For tipped employees, verify employer-paid wage plus tips equals at least $7.25/hour
Post the North Carolina Wage and Hour Notice in a conspicuous workplace location
Retain payroll records showing hours worked and wages paid for at least 3 years
